_about: Configuration for the GSL-SWY+ v1 pilot run on the Bear River basin (outlet = USGS 10126000 near Corinne). Values sourced from the monitor's lib/data.ts (single source of truth) plus the model design doc. Hypsometric band fractions are an approximate v1 discretization of the Bear basin above Corinne; the orographic precipitation multiplier and melt factor are calibrated against the gauge, so the band areas need only be approximately right.

lake._comment: South-arm (Saltair, gage 10010000) stage-area hypsometry; linearized near current stage. From lib/data.ts HYPSOMETRY.

routing._comment: Net basin yield reaching the lake via the Bear is reduced by upstream consumptive depletion + conveyance/reservoir losses, then lagged. The gauge at Corinne already integrates most upstream depletion, so for the v1 lake-stage conversion we treat gauge volume as the water delivered to the lake from the Bear.

seeding._comment: Cloud-seeding context from lib/data.ts SEEDING_CONTEXT. The paired model run adds a seeded precipitation increment to winter months in the snow-bearing bands.

seeded_precip_increment_pct._note: Fractional increase in cool-season (Nov-Mar) precipitation over seedable (snow-bearing) elevation bands during seeding operations. Range reflects published winter-orographic glaciogenic seeding effect sizes (~3-15% on precip in targeted storms/areas). Treated as an uncertain Monte Carlo parameter.

seeding._treatable_note: The published 3-10% increment is the effect in TARGETED storms over TARGETED areas, not a basin-wide season-long increase. The effective basin-wide cool-season precip bump is the increment times this treatable fraction = (targeted area share ~0.15-0.25) x (seedable-storm precip share ~0.15-0.20). 0.03 anchors the central physical yield (~4,700 AF at the outlet, ~1,170 AF to the lake) inside the operationally-validated full-deployment envelope (1,000-8,000 AF outlet; 250-2,000 AF to lake). Without it the physical pathway overstates the field-validated effect ~30x.
